title: Four-Pillar Gap Audit
intro: A health scare can reveal that ‘SIPs are running’ is not the same as ‘my family is protected.’ Rate your real-world pillars.

pillars.label: Protection
pillars.prompt: Life cover adequacy and health insurance that does not depend only on your current employer.
actions: List employer vs personal health cover gaps
Review nominees and policy documents this month

pillars.label: Growth
pillars.prompt: Long-term investments (equity/hybrid etc.) aligned to horizon — not idle cash pretending to be a plan.
actions: Confirm equity allocation matches time horizon
Avoid stopping SIPs for non-emergency noise
Rebalance only with a written rule

pillars.label: Liquidity
pillars.prompt: Emergency cash so you do not liquidate long-term assets (or take costly debt) under shock.
actions: Target 3–12 months of essential expenses in accessible form
Automate a top-up until the buffer exists

pillars.label: Goals
pillars.prompt: Named goals with rough amounts and timelines — education, retirement, house — not a nameless corpus.
actions: Write 2–4 goals with year and ballpark cost
Map each goal to a sleeve of money
Review once a year, not once a headline

debrief.summary: Growth without protection and liquidity is a plan that only works when life cooperates. Strengthen weak pillars before optimising fund picks.
